compiled_state.title: As a developer, I receive a .claude/project-understanding.md that captures everything the onboarding conversation learned
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Define the structure and generation logic for .claude/project-understanding.md — the lasting artifact that captures everything the onboarding conversation learned
**Why**: This artifact bridges /dso:onboarding to /dso:architect-foundation and serves as living project documentation, eliminating the need for re-detection or re-asking
**Scope**:
- IN: Structured markdown template with sections for stack, architecture, design summary, commands, infrastructure, CI, enforcement preferences; generation from conversation understanding; human-readable and editable format
- OUT: The conversation logic itself (S2); design-specific sections (S5); config file generation (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the onboarding conversation produces .claude/project-understanding.md with structured sections for stack, architecture, design, commands, infrastructure, and enforcement preferences
  ← Satisfies: "The conversation produces a structured markdown project understanding artifact"
- When this story is complete, the artifact is human-readable, editable, and usable as living project documentation that the team can consult and update
  ← Satisfies: "usable as both a skill-to-skill context bridge and living project documentation"
- When this story is complete, the artifact contains all detected-and-confirmed findings alongside user-provided context, with clear attribution of source (detected vs. user-stated)
  ← Satisfies: "capturing all detected and user-confirmed details"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Template structure should be stable — /dso:architect-foundation will parse it; document the section contract

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks closed. Story complete: .claude/project-understanding.md artifact generation with attribution and structured sections.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
